banco_oraciones.oracion: Sofía can speak French, and so can Kenji.
Ben wanted to leave early, and so did Kenji.
She finished her coffee and then did the same with mine.
Sofía doesn't like early meetings, and neither does Ben.
Do you think it will rain? I hope not.
Can Sofía speak French? I think so.
Did Ben want to leave early? I believe so.
Will it rain tomorrow? I hope not.
Can Kenji speak French too? Yes, so can he.
Did Ben want to leave? Yes, and so did Kenji.

interaccion: Kenji: Sofía can speak French, **and so can I**, actually.
Ben: Really? I had no idea. Will it rain later?
Sofía: **I hope not**, we have plans outside.
Abuela Carmen: **I think so**, actually. Bring an umbrella. I always do.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Sofía can speak French, and Kenji can too so.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: Sofía can speak French, and so can Kenji.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Ellipsis and substitution let you avoid repeating a whole verb phrase — 'so can I', 'I think so', 'neither does she' — by replacing it with a short, economical structure.

filas: Sofía can speak French, and so can Kenji.
Sofía doesn't like meetings, and neither does Ben.
Will it rain? I hope not. / I think so.

ejercicios.pregunta: Sofía can speak French, and ___ Kenji.
Sofía doesn't like early meetings, and ___ Ben.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'So can I' repeats the full verb phrase instead of shortening it.
'I think so' is a short way of avoiding repeating a whole clause.
'Neither does she' is used to agree with a negative statement.

ejercicios.texto: Ben wanted to leave early, and ___ Kenji.
Can Sofía speak French? I think ___.

ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía can speak French and so can Kenji
Sofía doesn't like early meetings and neither does Ben

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Sofía can speak French, and Kenji can too so.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía can speak French, and so can Kenji.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Sofía can speak French. Kenji can speak French too.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía can speak French, and so can Kenji.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Sofía doesn't like early meetings. Ben doesn't like them either.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía doesn't like early meetings, and neither does Ben.

ejercicios.instruccion: Answer briefly expressing hope that it won't.
Write the negative form (you don't believe it).

ejercicios.pregunta: Do you think English gets easier at C1? Answer briefly.

guion: Kenji: Sofía can speak French, and so can I, actually.
Ben: Really? I had no idea. Will it rain later?
Sofía: I hope not, we have plans outside.
Abuela Carmen: I think so, actually. Bring an umbrella.
Ben: Sofía doesn't trust the forecast, and neither do I anymore.
Kenji: Neither does anyone in this group, honestly.

comprension.pregunta: Who else can speak French, besides Sofía?
What does Abuela Carmen think will happen?

reading.texto: Nobody in the group fully trusts the weather forecast anymore, and neither, frankly, does Abuela Carmen — though she's usually right anyway. 'Will it rain today?' Kenji asked before their walk. 'I think so,' she said, already reaching for her umbrella. Sofía hoped not; she had plans that only worked if it stayed dry. It didn't. Ben, who'd left his umbrella at home again, got soaked — and so, predictably, did his phone, safely tucked in his pocket as always. 'I told you so,' Abuela Carmen didn't say, mostly because she didn't need to.

preguntas.afirmacion: Abuela Carmen trusts the forecast completely.
Abuela Carmen said 'I told you so' out loud.
